incorrects.description: Student tapped a wrong rectangle on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Find the area of each rectangle first, then check if it is a multiple of 3.

incorrects.description: Student tapped a wrong rectangle on second attempt

steps.text: Look at this rectangle. It has 3 rows of 4 squares. Count the total: 3 rows of 4 is 12. Is 12 a multiple of 3?

incorrects.description: Student tapped a wrong rectangle three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: 3×4 rectangle highlights to show the correct answer.

steps.text: Let me show you. The 3×4 rectangle has 3 rows of 4 squares. That gives an area of 12. 12 is a multiple of 3 because 3 times 4 equals 12. The answer is the 3×4 rectangle.
